text:
Day seven of theParis2024Olympicsis in full swing withTeam GBadding seven medals to their ever-increasing haul.

Emily Craig and Imogen Grantwon a thrilling gold in the lightweight women’s double sculls, while Bryony Page wasalso victorious in the trampoline final.

Their excellent wins add to a silver medal for Ollie Wynne-Griffiths and Tom George, whonarrowly missed out on winning the men’s pairs.

Ben Maher, Scott Brash and Harry Charles won gold in the team jumping final – the latteremulating his father’s achievement from London 2012.

Jack Laugher and Anthony Harding alsowon bronze in the men’s 3m springboard synchronised diving final.

In the evening, Ben Proud and Duncan Scott added more silver medals from the swimming pool.

All three of Britain’s medals on day six of the Gamescame courtesy of the rowers yesterday.

Athletics events have started at the Stade de France with British sprint star Dina Asher-Smith qualifying for the women’s 100m semi-final, ahead of the men’s 10,000m final.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

Another great day of Olympic action is over but before we go here’s what to look out for tomorrow.

See you in the morning!

One week into the Olympics and Team GB are fifth in the medal table.

Seven more medals on Day 7 including three golds ends another brilliant day of action for the Brits.

Just as it was at Wimbledon earlier this summer, it’ll be Novak Djokovic against Carlos Alcaraz in the final of the men’s tennis singles.

The Serbian beat Italy’s Lorenzo Musetti 6-4, 6-2 at Roland Garros.

The Olympics is one of the few tournaments Djokovic has not won. He’ll go for gold on Sunday.

Oh dear. Beth Shriever didn’t get her typical blistering start and the door was slammed shut on her.

The Brit fell to the back of the pack and couldn’t recover, finishing last.

Australia’s Saya Sakakibara takes gold.

In better news from the BMX track, Team GB’s Beth Shriever is in the final of the women’s racing.

She won all three of her semi-finals and is the defending Olympic champion.

Time for one more British gold tonight?

GB Cycling have released the following statement on Kye Whyte:

‘Following his crash in tonight’s BMX racing semi-final, Kye Whyte was assessed immediately by the on-site medical team. This was followed up by further immediate review by Great Britain Cycling Team doctor Nigel Jones.

‘Initial reports are positive but he is being taken to hospital for further assessment.

‘We wish Kye a speedy recovery and look forward to seeing him on his bike very soon.’

Worrying news from the BMX track after Brit Ky Whyte was stretchered away after a nasty crash.

We’ll keep you updated when we get more news.

More silver! Back-to-back silver medals for Duncan Scott in the men’s 200m individual medley – his eighth Olympic medal.

The home fans only care about one man though – France superstar Leon Marchand won gold by over a second and sets a new Olympic record in the process.

So close! Ben Proud does win a medal in the men’s 50m freestyle.

It’s silver but he was so, so, so close to a gold. Australia’s Cameron McEvoy wins gold by just 0.05 seconds.

Let’s go to the swimming pool now where Team GB can add to their medal haul for the day.

Up first, Ben Proud in the men’s 50m freestyle…

Team GB’s Keely Hodgkinson, Jemma Reekie and Phoebe Gill all qualified with relative ease to the semi-finals of the women’s 800m.

Hodgkinson is favourite for the gold but huge praise must go to 17-year-old Gill who has a very bright future ahead of her.

Huang Yaqiong won gold in the badminton mixed doubles for China.

But she’ll be leaving Paris not only with a medal but a diamond ring after teammate Liu Yuchen proposed after her triumphant match.

Congratulations to the happy couple!

The British quartet of Sam Reardon, Laviai Nielsen, Alex Haydock-Wilson and Nicole Yeargin have qualified for the final of the 4x400m mixed relay.

They did so in style, setting a new British record in the process. The final takes place tomorrow night.

Fresh off beating England to win Euro 2024 (no I’m still not over it) Spain are looking to triumph in a second football tournament this summer.

A 3-0 victory over Japan sees La Roja advance to the semi-finals where they’ll play Morocco who thrashed the USA 4-0 earlier today.

On the other side of the draw, Egypt take on Paraguay before the big one – hosts France against Argentina in what could be a fiery affair…

Judo superstar Teddy Riner lit the Olympic cauldron and has now won his fourth gold medal in his final games.

The Frenchman’s victory was enjoyed by none other than President Emmanuel Macron who the pair sharing a passionate hug.

Harry Charles won gold forTeam GBin the team jumping inParis– 12 years after his father Peter won the same event atLondon 2012.

Remarkable both Harry and Peter won with the same teammates: Ben Maher and Scott Brash.

‘I cannot believe it, last time we won gold these two boys were there with my dad. They have been heroes of mine and to be up next to them on the podium is unbelievable.’ Read more here:

Yusuf Dikec became a viral sensation after his extremely laid back approach in the shooting helped win Turkey a silver medal.

The 51-year-old was likened to a movie hitman after competing with his hand in his pocket and no eye or ear protection that all his rivals used.

‘I never needed that equipment. I am a natural. A natural shooter.’

Just a day after agonisingly missing out on a medal in the kayak slalom, Joe Clarke has put himself in good contention to win a new Olympic sport: kayak cross.

A time of 66.08 seconds in the qualifiers means he’ll be top seed when the racing gets underway tomorrow, making his route to gold (theoretically) easier.

Team GB’s other star in the men’s event, Adam Burgess, is seeded 26th of 38 competitors.

Lin Yu-ting has become the second boxer to win inParisafter failing a gender eligibility test last year.

The Taiwanese boxer beat Uzbekistan’s Sitora Turdibekova by unanimous decision in the 57kg category.

The 28-year-old’s participation at the Olympics in Paris has come under scrutiny as she was disqualified from the Women’s World Championships last year for failing International Boxing Association (IBA) eligibility rules which prevents athletes with XY chromosomes from participating in women’s events.

Tommy Fleetwood has just finished his second round on the golf course, carding a brilliant seven-under-par to take the joint-lead.

The Team GB star is on 11-under overall, tied with Japan’s Hideki Matsuyama and USA’s Xander Schauffele.

The other Brit, Matt Fitzpatrick, has also improved on day two, moving onto five-under-par. Ireland’s Rory McIlroy is lagging behind on four-under.

ArrowMORE:Olympic badminton star gets engaged to teammate just moments after winning gold

ArrowMORE:Lin Yu-ting becomes second Olympic boxer to win after failed gender test

title: Olympics schedule and results: Athletics, gymnastics and tennis | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 7 (August 2) schedule: Athletics, gymnastics, swimming, tennis and boxing.
